methodsAttempted.query: site:osu.edu Ohio State University generative AI policy academic integrity students ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official Ohio State AI responsible-use, ASC course-policy, Drake/teaching, and OAA pages. Search snippets were used only for discovery.
A follow-up Firecrawl search returned a 402 credit error, so discovery continued through official links and direct HTTP fetches rather than repeated Firecrawl retries.

Official OSU AI and teaching pages linked to security/privacy, academic conduct, approved tools, and course-policy resources. Only public pages that resolved without access controls were used as evidence.
Robots files for ai.osu.edu, teaching.resources.osu.edu, ascode.osu.edu, and it.osu.edu did not disallow the fetched public content paths.

artifacts.summary: The crawl found multiple public official Ohio State pages with direct AI guidance: the university AI responsible-use page, Arts and Sciences course-policy guidance, the Teaching and Learning Resource Center AI teaching guidance, and OTDI security/privacy guidance. A legacy OAA AI academic-integrity URL redirected to a general misconduct page and was not used as AI-specific evidence.

artifacts.verificationNotes: The legacy AI academic-integrity URL redirected to a general Committee on Academic Misconduct page that did not provide direct AI-specific evidence during this crawl. AI-specific claims use other official OSU pages instead.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The fetched final page was titled Committee on Academic Misconduct and did not expose direct AI-specific policy text in the normalized public content captured for this run.
artifacts.notes: Retained as a rejected discovery path because official OSU pages referred to academic conduct guidance, but this redirected page was too generic for AI-specific claims.

targets.sourceTitle: Responsible Use | Artificial Intelligence at Ohio State
Crafting Policy for Student Use of Artificial Intelligence | ASC Office of Distance Education
AI Considerations for Teaching and Learning | Teaching and Learning Resource Center
Security and Privacy Statement on Artificial Intelligence | Office of Technology and Digital Innovation

stopConditions: Stop after public official Ohio State pages support narrow claim candidates.
Do not infer university-wide binding policy from sample syllabus language; scope claims to the source audience and wording.
Do not use search snippets, news-only items, third-party pages, vendor pages, or pages behind access controls as evidence.
Do not bypass login walls, robots restrictions, WAF, CAPTCHA, or paywalls.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Crafting Policy for Student Use of Artificial Intelligence | ASC Office of Distance Education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: To maintain a culture of integrity and respect, these generative AI tools should not be used in the completion of course assignments unless an instructor for a given course specifically authorizes their use. Some instructors may approve of using generative AI tools in the academic setting for specific goals. However, these tools should be used only with the explicit and clear permission of each individual instructor, and then only in the ways allowed by the instructor.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI Considerations for Teaching and Learning | Teaching and Learning Resource Center
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It's important to establish expectations for academic integrity--both in general and specific to AI use--early in the term. Include university policies for academic integrity in your syllabus. Openly communicating the university's policies for academic integrity in your syllabus, as well as your own policy for GenAI use, will level set expectations for your course as well as for students' academic careers at the university.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Security and Privacy Statement on Artificial Intelligence | Office of Technology and Digital Innovation
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: While it is a best practice to only use S1 (public) or S2 (internal) institutional data in approved AI tools, S3 (private) and/or S4 (restricted) data can be included when necessary for your education, business or research use case. Keep in mind that all software carries a risk of data breaches, so only enter the data necessary to achieve your goal. University community members should not enter any institutional data that is categorized above the S1 (public) level into unvetted AI tools.

artifacts.title: The Ohio State University AI policy source discovery draft
artifacts.summary: Official Ohio State sources support narrow candidate claims about instructor permission for student GenAI coursework use, COAM-linked academic integrity guidance, instructor communication of course AI expectations, vetted AI tools, and institutional-data handling for approved and unvetted AI tools. A legacy OAA AI academic-integrity URL redirected to a generic misconduct page and was not used as AI-specific evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged draft is not a canonical publication; OpenClaw candidates require downstream review before public release.
Claims are scoped to the public official pages fetched on 2026-05-15 and do not infer rules for every course beyond instructor-specific guidance.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
